Politics.headline: MPs call for joined-up approach and one-stop shop for SMEs to support economic growth across Northern Ireland
Politics.detail: The Northern Ireland Affairs Committee has published a report calling for the UK Government to align its economic strategy for Northern Ireland with that of the NI Executive, and to provide greater support for small and medium-sized enterprises.

Politics.headline: IDC report finds Govt losing hard-won gains for women and girls as aid cuts bite
Politics.detail: The International Development Committee found that the UK government is losing progress in global gender equality due to aid cuts and inaction, with the full impact on women and girls expected to be detrimental.

Politics.headline: Trump says world gone crazy as he meets Macron at Notre Dame reopening
Politics.detail: Donald Trump met with Emmanuel Macron at the reopening of Notre Dame, commenting that the world has gone crazy.

Politics.headline: Switzerland halts US arms exports amid Iran war, citing neutrality
Politics.detail: The Swiss government has halted exports of U.S. arms and closed its airspace to U.S. military flights directly linked to the Iran war, citing its neutral status.

Politics.headline: 'They want to colonise us': Brazil's Lula warns of foreign interference
Politics.detail: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized U.S. actions in Latin America, though he did not name Trump, and warned of foreign interference.

Finance & Economy.headline: 'The stakes are enormous': how a prolonged Iran war could shock the global economy
Finance & Economy.detail: Donald Trump's 'little excursion' is likely to have long-term effects, from oil prices to inflation to growth, say experts, as the Iran war continues to disrupt the global economy. The conflict has led to a sharp rise in prices of oil and gas, shipping, air freight costs, and fertilizers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Energy shock talk grabs headlines but the Iran war is also driving the world towards a food crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing Iran war is not only causing an energy crisis but also threatening a global food crisis due to disruptions in Middle Eastern fertilizer shipments, potentially diminishing crop yields and increasing food prices. This could exacerbate economic and food security challenges, especially in developing countries.

Finance & Economy.headline: As Iran war disrupts the Gulf, India's growth story faces new risks
Finance & Economy.detail: The disruption in the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war could significantly impact India's LPG supplies and refined product markets, making them more vulnerable than crude oil markets. PM Modi has chaired a CCS meet on the West Asia crisis, ordering steps to shield India from the impact.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK Manufacturers face rising energy costs amid Middle East conflict
Finance & Economy.detail: The Middle East conflict is driving up energy costs and threatening further supply chain disruptions, adding to existing cost pressures on manufacturers. The CBI senior economist, Cameron Martin, warned about the impact of the conflict on manufacturers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Energy Watchdog suggests working from home amid oil crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: Due to the ongoing oil crisis, the Energy Watchdog has suggested that people should work from home and avoid flying. This recommendation comes as borrowing costs soar to post-financial crisis highs, further impacting the economy.

Technology.headline: Data#3 named HP Greater Asia Top Amplify Impact Partner
Technology.detail: Data#3, an Australian technology services and solutions provider, has been recognized as HP Greater Asia Top Amplify Impact Partner for 2025, combining technical expertise with a commitment to sustainability and responsible lifecycle management. The award highlights their strong customer engagement and shared vision with HP for a greener future.

Technology.headline: Hitachi Receives the 2026 Catalyst Award, a Global Recognition for Building an Inclusive Organization
Technology.detail: Hitachi has been awarded the 2026 Catalyst Award, recognizing the company's commitment to building an inclusive organization. The award acknowledges Hitachi's efforts in promoting diversity and inclusion within its global operations.

Technology.headline: New data centre expectations help bring the benefit of AI to all Australians
Technology.detail: The Australian Government has set out expectations for data center and AI infrastructure projects, focusing on national interests like job creation, energy grid improvements, and adherence to privacy and security laws. These expectations are part of the National AI Plan and aim to ensure that data center development aligns with Australia's needs.

Technology.headline: Gartner Survey Reveals Acquiring and Developing AI and Digital Talent is CFOs' Top Near-Term Challenge
Technology.detail: A Gartner survey of CFOs identifies building AI talent and responding to unpredictable market conditions as top challenges in the next six months. CFOs are encouraged to upskill their existing workforce to close digital capability gaps and drive more value from current tools.

Technology.headline: Google outlines new phase of AI in healthcare research at The Check Up event
Technology.detail: Google has outlined a new phase of AI in healthcare research at The Check Up event. The company is furthering its efforts to apply artificial intelligence to improve healthcare outcomes.

Science.headline: Scientists Crack How Mosquitoes Decide Where to Fly
Science.detail: Mosquitoes don't follow each other; instead, they independently react to visual cues and carbon dioxide, targeting humans when both signals align. Dark colors and CO2 exhalation lead them to humans.

Science.headline: Beavers Could Be Climate Heroes
Science.detail: New research suggests beavers could play a powerful role in fighting climate change by building dams and transforming streams into wetlands, which dramatically reshape how carbon moves. Beaver-built wetlands can trap and store large amounts of carbon, offering a low-cost boost for restoration and climate resilience.

Science.headline: World's Oldest Known Cave Art Discovered
Science.detail: Researchers have uncovered the world's oldest known cave art, a 67,800-year-old hand stencil in Indonesia, hinting at early symbolic thinking and possibly spiritual beliefs. The unusual, claw-like design strengthens the case for early human artistic expression.

Science.headline: Earth's Lithosphere was Already Segmented and Mobile 3.5 Billion Years Ago
Science.detail: A recent geology study indicates that Earth's lithosphere was already segmented and mobile 3.5 billion years ago. This suggests that plate tectonics may have started earlier than previously thought.

Science.headline: GLP-1 Medications May Offer Mental Health Benefits
Science.detail: GLP-1 medications like semaglutide (Ozempic) may offer unexpected mental health benefits alongside weight loss, with a large study finding major drops in depression, anxiety, and psychiatric-related hospital visits among users. These medications could potentially improve both physical and mental well-being.

Health.headline: UK authorities revise meningitis outbreak cases to 29
Health.detail: British health authorities have revised the number of confirmed and likely cases from a meningitis outbreak in southeast England to 29, from a figure of 34 given previously; the outbreak has claimed the lives of two people.

Health.headline: Two hikers among group of 14 rescued from B.C. mountain, one critical condition
Health.detail: Two hikers were rescued from Mount Brunswick after falling in snowy conditions, with one in critical condition; the rescue involved helicopters and rope systems in steep terrain.

Health.headline: Study links children's social media use with anxiety and depression in teenage years
Health.detail: Researchers have found a link between children's social media use and increased anxiety and depression in teenage years, suggesting that lack of sleep could be a contributing factor for young people interacting online for more than three hours a day.

Health.headline: Sarawak reviewing strategies to strengthen rabies control
Health.detail: Sarawak is reviewing its strategies to strengthen rabies control efforts in the region.

Health.headline: It's not just vaccines — parents are refusing other routine preventive care for newborns
Health.detail: Parents are increasingly refusing routine preventative care, not just vaccines, for their newborns.

Sports.headline: Hurricanes score 3 time on the power play in a 5-1 win over the Penguins
Sports.detail: The Carolina Hurricanes capitalized on power play opportunities to secure a dominant 5-1 victory over the Pittsburgh Penguins.

Sports.headline: Man City dominates Arsenal to win English League Cup and make statement in Premier League title race
Sports.detail: Manchester City defeated Arsenal 2-0 in the English League Cup final at Wembley Stadium, asserting their dominance in the Premier League title race.

Sports.headline: Tyler Reddick wins at Darlington in 4th NASCAR win this season for Michael Jordan's team
Sports.detail: Tyler Reddick secured his fourth NASCAR win of the season at Darlington, marking another victory for Michael Jordan's team.

Sports.headline: EASL Finals: B.League's Utsunomiya Brex claim crown
Sports.detail: Utsunomiya Brex from the B.League emerged victorious in the EASL Finals, claiming the championship title.

Sports.headline: PBA: Bossing pin third straight defeat on Hotshots
Sports.detail: The Bossing handed the Hotshots their third consecutive defeat in the PBA (Philippine Basketball Association).

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris' Health: Why Was He Rushed to a Hospital in 2026?
Entertainment.detail: Norris was reportedly hospitalized in Hawaii in mid-March 2026; details about his health prior to his death have emerged.

Entertainment.headline: Celebrities Who Own Homes in Hawaii Amid 2026 Floods: Jason Momoa & More
Entertainment.detail: Jason Momoa shared photos and videos from the catastrophic floods that hit Hawaii in late March 2026, shining a light on other celebrities who own property on the islands.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026 recap: 'One Battle After Another' wins best picture after big night
Entertainment.detail: Paul Thomas Anderson's “One Battle After Another” took home six Oscars, including best picture, while Michael B. Jordan emerged victorious for his leading role in “Sinners,” and Jessie Buckley won best actress for “Hamnet”.

Entertainment.headline: TWICE reflect on a decade as a group: 'There's so much more we can do' | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: Speaking backstage at the Kia Forum, TWICE members reflect on their 10th anniversary release, solo tracks, evolving definitions of success, and contributing to the Netflix hit “KPop Demon Hunters”.

Entertainment.headline: Justin Timberlake's DWI Case: About His Arrest, Charges, Guilty Plea & Police Bodycam Video
Entertainment.detail: Nearly two years after his DWI arrest, police bodycam footage from Timberlake's encounter with police was released to the public.

Crime & Justice.headline: Peers vote to back clause pardoning women convicted over illegal abortions
Crime & Justice.detail: The UK House of Lords has voted to support a clause that would pardon women convicted of illegal abortions in the past.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man accused of shooting father in face with crossbow captured after snowy manhunt
Crime & Justice.detail: A West Virginia man was arrested after allegedly shooting his father in the face with a crossbow and leading police on a snowy multicounty manhunt.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cop's skull smashed in creek fight as repeat offender suspect walks on $100 bond
Crime & Justice.detail: A Colorado police officer suffered a serious head injury in a violent struggle with an accused drug dealer, but the suspect was later released on a bond of just $100.

Crime & Justice.headline: Fentanyl found inside Barbies sold at Missouri discount store, police say
Crime & Justice.detail: Police found that five Barbie packages containing fentanyl were sold at a Missouri discount store, but they have all been recovered.

Crime & Justice.headline: "American Nightmare" survivor Denise Huskins' new discovery exposes victim privacy loophole in California law
Crime & Justice.detail: The failure to protect explicit case evidence in Denise Huskins' kidnapping and sexual assault case is driving reform at the State Capitol and exposed a gap in state law that could expose videos of sexual assault victims.

Education.headline: Education Bill sparks power clash fears
Education.detail: Unclear lines of authority in the proposed Education Bill are raising concerns about governance and accountability as the bill leaves key operational boundaries to regulations instead of law, risking overlapping authority.

Education.headline: Education Ministry extends e-learning amid ongoing regional escalation
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education and Higher Education announced that e-learning will continue through the end of this week, until Thursday, across public and private schools as well as kindergartens, while high school students will continue attending classes in person only.

Education.headline: Lecture room as tapestry: weaving culture, curiosity and learning together
Education.detail: Culturally inclusive pedagogy asks educators to redesign learning environments that work with, rather than ignore, the cultural diversity in classrooms, challenging unspoken norms and creating spaces where students' identities and experiences enhance academic work.

Education.headline: A colourful approach to public education
Education.detail: Project Rangichangi is transforming government schools in Nepal with murals, creating joyful spaces for learning while empowering young students.

Education.headline: Education News Today – 23 March, 2026
Education.detail: CBSE Class 12 students are preparing for their Political Science exam scheduled for tomorrow, March 24, and are advised to revise key concepts; IP University has launched a new M.Tech course in Robotics and AI for the 2026 intake.

Lifestyle.headline: People 'bathe' in nature to get respite from chaotic news cycle
Lifestyle.detail: People are seeking refuge in nature to escape the overwhelming news cycle, with activities like forest bathing gaining popularity as a way to unwind and appreciate the environment.

Lifestyle.headline: 7 High-Paying Remote Jobs That Can Fund A Digital Nomad Lifestyle In 2026
Lifestyle.detail: More individuals are establishing income sources that can move with them, earning differently to facilitate a more flexible, global lifestyle through remote work.

Lifestyle.headline: Yoga With Swami Ramdev : India will become number 1 in the Happiness Index on the strength of Yoga. - India TV News
Lifestyle.detail: Swami Ramdev emphasizes that India can achieve the top position in the Happiness Index through yoga, which ensures a healthy body and a calm mind, recommending simple asanas and pranayama for stress reduction and sustained happiness.

Lifestyle.headline: The Lucky Shoes Of The Late, Great Parachutist Joe Kittinger
Lifestyle.detail: A first-person account describes the eye-opening experience of a tandem parachute jump.

Lifestyle.headline: From breakup texts to 'I do': How AI is reshaping modern relationships
Lifestyle.detail: Utahns are increasingly using artificial intelligence tools not just for tasks, but also for connection, from breakup assistance to relationship building.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Why Poland really became a top 20 economy
The ugly truth about 'merit' promotion in the federal government
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Letters to the Editor discuss merit promotion in the federal government.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Behind the scenes, a compromise on TSA takes shape
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The TSA line on Sunday at John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The case against the Senate filibuster no Republican can make
Opinion/Editorial.detail: How to put Congress, not the president, in the U.S. politics driver's seat: End the 60-vote tradition.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Bluesky raises $100M but faces a messy reality
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Illustration by Washington Post staff/The Washington Post; Mario Tama/Getty Images

World.headline: Trump Orders ICE Agents to Airports Amid Crippling Budget Standoff
World.detail: Amid a budget standoff, President Trump has ordered ICE agents to airports, prompting officials to quickly develop a plan, which is causing confusion and long lines at airports.

World.headline: Iran Threatens Strikes on Gulf Energy as Trump's 48-Hour Deadline Looms
World.detail: With Trump's 48-hour deadline for reopening the Strait of Hormuz approaching, Iran has threatened to strike energy facilities in the Gulf, raising concerns about a potential deepening of the regional crisis and its impact on global markets.

World.headline: Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire Elected Mayor of Paris
World.detail: City hall veteran Emmanuel Grégoire defeated rightwinger Rachida Dati in the Paris mayoral race, while Marseille's leftist incumbent also won against a far-right opponent.

World.headline: Russia Claims Control of Border Village in Ukraine's Sumy Region
World.detail: Amid the ongoing conflict, Russia has claimed to have taken control of a border village located in the Sumy region of Ukraine.

World.headline: Water emerges as a dangerous new war target
World.detail: After damage to its water and energy infrastructure, Iran has threatened to target desalination plants if its own infrastructure is attacked, raising concerns about water systems becoming targets in wartime.

Zimbabwe.headline: All set for national heroes' triple burial
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa will today preside over the burial of three national heroes, Major-General (Retired) Herbert Chingumwe, Brigadier General (Retired) Fakazi Mleya and Mr. Abraham James Kabasa at the National Heroes Acre in Harare.

Zimbabwe.headline: New grain sourcing rule cuts imports
Zimbabwe.detail: Millers, stockfeed manufacturers and food processors will from next week be required to source at least 70 percent of their grain requirements from local farmers and contractors, a move that is expected to boost domestic production and reduce reliance on imports.

Zimbabwe.headline: Job evaluation paves way for health sector salary review
Zimbabwe.detail: The Health Service Commission has completed a comprehensive job evaluation exercise for health workers, paving the way for a salary review aimed at improving their remuneration and working conditions.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chiefs fall to determined MWOS
Zimbabwe.detail: MWOS defeated Bulawayo Chiefs 2-1 in a recent match, with Lloyd Mutasa seemingly finding a solution to his team's challenges. The match took place in Norton.

Zimbabwe.headline: Dams in Mzingwane catchment spilling: Zinwa
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe National Water Authority (ZINWA) has issued a warning to stakeholders and communities in and around Gwanda, stating that major dams in the Mzingwane Catchment area are spilling due to recent heavy rainfall, with communities downstream urged to exercise caution. Mtshabezi Dam is currently spilling, and Inzisa Dam is expected to follow suit if current inflows continue.
